sentence_reviews.text: Structural causal models, interventions, counterfactuals, and confounding distinguish correlation from causal effect.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Causal model page definition, ladder of causation, and counterfactuals sections; Causal inference page definition and concerns about correlative results; Rubin causal model page conclusion
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected sources support structural equations/models, interventions, counterfactual or potential-outcome reasoning, assumptions, and the distinction between correlation/association and causation. The sentence is a compact synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: The role of Causal inference is to connect Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design to downstream capabilities including Causal representation learning, Knowledge editing & memory consolidation, Fairness, bias & subgroup analysis.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html lines 543-560 and 745-749; Causal inference page definition, approaches, and framework sections; Causal model page applications and ladder sections
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support causal inference as statistical/probabilistic and relevant to machine learning and bias/fairness-adjacent analysis. They do not directly support the graph-derived downstream list, especially knowledge editing and memory consolidation.

sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Causal model page applications section mentioning machine learning and causal models
sentence_reviews.notes: Causal models are connected to machine learning, but the inspected evidence does not directly establish causal representation learning as a downstream capability of causal inference.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Knowledge editing & memory consolidation
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected evidence supports knowledge representation and probabilistic/statistical learning, but it does not connect causal inference to knowledge editing or memory consolidation.

sentence_reviews.text: substrate for Fairness, bias & subgroup analysis
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: AIMA contents.html lines 745-749; Causal inference page definition and concerns about misuse of regression/correlative results
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support fairness/bias as AI topics and causal inference as a way to avoid confusing correlation with cause. They do not directly establish the named downstream relationship.

sentence_reviews.text: Its abstractions require explicit assumptions; a mathematically valid result can still be irrelevant to a mismatched empirical setting. Its direct dependencies include Probability theory, Statistics & experimental design.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Causal inference page concerns and common frameworks sections; Rubin causal model conclusion; AIMA contents.html lines 543-560 and 768-774
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support assumptions and statistical/probabilistic dependencies. The broad warning about mathematically valid results being irrelevant in mismatched empirical settings is plausible but not directly stated in the inspected evidence.

sentence_reviews.text: Causal inference rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Probability theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Causal model page association and do-calculus sections; Causal inference page see-also/common frameworks; AIMA contents.html lines 543-560 and 773-774
sentence_reviews.notes: The evidence connects causal inference to probabilities, observational data, statistical learning, and probabilistic models. The exact dependency wording is atlas synthesis.

sentence_reviews.text: Causal inference rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Statistics & experimental design.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Causal inference page approaches and experimental/random assignment discussion; Rubin causal model conclusion and further reading
sentence_reviews.notes: The sources support causal inference as tied to randomized/observational study design, statistical methods, and assumptions for estimating causal effects.

sentence_reviews.text: Causal inference rests on the formal or computational capabilities supplied by Graph theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Do-calculus page definition and purpose section; Causal inference page common frameworks section
sentence_reviews.notes: The inspected sources support causal graphs and DAG-based graphical criteria in do-calculus, but graph theory is not universal to all causal-inference frameworks. Common strength is more defensible than core.

edge_reviews.notes: Probability is central to associations, observational probabilities, and probabilistic/statistical causal methods. The edge direction and requires relationship are defensible.
Causal inference relies heavily on statistical inference, study design, randomized assignment, and assumptions for estimating counterfactuals. Core strength is defensible.
Graphical causal models and do-calculus use DAGs and graphical criteria, but not all causal inference is graph-based. The common strength is appropriate.
